div1.entryFree:
Clip, 1) to cut with shears: Judas Macca-baeus clipt is plain Judas, LLL V, 2, 603. this ornament . . . will I c. to form, Per. V, 3, 74. Hence == to curtail, diminish: nor more nor --ed, but so, Lr. IV, 7, 6.
2) to embrace, surround: to c. Elysium, and to lack her joy, Ven. 600. she --ed Adonis in her arms, Pilgr. 148. Pilgr. 148 Wint. V, 2, 59. Cor. I, 6, 29. IV, 5, 115. Ant. IV, 8, 8. Cymb. V, 5, 451. Neptune's arms who --eth thee about, John V, 2, 34. --ed in with the sea, H4A III, 1, 44. no grave shall c. in it a pair so famous, Ant. V, 2, 362. you elements that c. us round about, Oth. III, 3, 464. his meanest garment, that ever hath but --ed his body, Cymb. II, 3, 139. Dubious passage: who with their drowsy, slow and flagging wings c. dead men's graves, H6B IV, 1, 6 (Ff. cleap).*
3) see Clepe.
